title: JEE Advanced Syllabus 2026
subject: JEE Advanced Syllabus
content:
# JEE Advanced 2026 Official Syllabus

Conducted by IIT Roorkee, the JEE Advanced syllabus focuses on depth and application of concepts. It includes additional topics not present in JEE Main.

## 1. Physics

### General Physics
- Units and Dimensions
- Error analysis (least count, significant figures)
- Experimental skills (Vernier calipers, Screw gauge, etc.)

### Mechanics
- Kinematics in 1D and 2D
- Newton's Laws of Motion
- Work, Energy, and Power
- Centre of Mass, Conservation of Momentum
- Impulse and Collisions
- **Rotational Motion:** Moment of Inertia, Parallel and Perpendicular axes theorems, Rolling without slipping, Angular momentum conservation.
- **Gravitation:** Motion of planets and satellites.
- **Fluid Mechanics:** Pressure, Buoyancy, Surface Tension, Viscosity, Bernoulli's Theorem.

### Thermal Physics
- Thermal expansion, Calorimetry, Latent heat.
- Heat transfer (Conduction, Convection, Radiation, Newton's law of cooling).
- First and Second laws of Thermodynamics.
- Carnot cycle.
- Kinetic theory of gases.

### Electricity and Magnetism
- Electrostatics, Gauss's Law.
- Capacitance and Dielectrics.
- Current Electricity (Ohm's Law, Kirchhoff's Laws, Potentiometer).
- Magnetic effect of current (Biot-Savart Law, Ampere's Law).
- Force on a moving charge/current-carrying wire.
- Electromagnetic Induction (Faraday's Law, Lenz's Law).
- Alternating Currents.

### Optics
- Ray Optics (Reflection, Refraction, Lenses, Prisms, Optical Instruments).
- Wave Optics (Interference, Diffraction, **Polarization**).

### Modern Physics
- Atomic Structure (Bohr's Model, X-rays).
- Dual nature of matter and radiation.
- Nuclear Physics (Radioactivity, Fission, Fusion).

---

## 2. Chemistry

### Physical Chemistry
- Atomic Structure
- Chemical Bonding
- Gaseous and Liquid States
- Energetics (Thermodynamics & Thermochemistry)
- Chemical and Ionic Equilibrium
- Electrochemistry
- Chemical Kinetics
- Solid State
- Solutions
- Surface Chemistry

### Inorganic Chemistry
- Isolation of Metals
- p-Block Elements
- d- and f-Block Elements
- Coordination Compounds
- Qualitative Analysis

### Organic Chemistry
- Basic Principles (Isomerism, GOC)
- Hydrocarbons (Alkanes, Alkenes, Alkynes)
- Benzene and its derivatives
- Alcohols, Phenols, Ethers
- Aldehydes and Ketones
- Carboxylic Acids and derivatives
- Amines
- Biomolecules (Carbohydrates, Amino acids, Proteins)
- Polymers

---

## 3. Mathematics

### Algebra
- Complex Numbers
- Quadratic Equations
- Progressions (AP, GP, HP)
- Logarithms
- Permutations and Combinations
- Binomial Theorem
- Matrices and Determinants
- Probability (Bayes' Theorem)

### Trigonometry
- Trigonometric functions and identities
- Inverse trigonometric functions
- Properties of Triangles

### Analytical Geometry
- Straight Lines
- Circles
- Conic Sections (Parabola, Ellipse, Hyperbola)

### Differential Calculus
- Functions, Limits, Continuity
- Differentiation, Chain Rule
- Applications of Derivatives (Tangent/Normal, Maxima/Minima, Mean Value Theorems)

### Integral Calculus
- Indefinite and Definite Integrals
- Area bounded by curves
- Differential Equations

### Vectors
- Vector addition, Dot product, Cross product, Scalar and Vector Triple products.
